title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends on fuel transition, shipyard actions, government, ports, financing and industry players
One of the world's biggest emitters of greenhouse gases, India aims to cut emissions to net zero by 2070, and the shipping minister said three of its ports would initially have bunker facilities for green hydrogen and ammonia. Read More

Between The Promise of Tomorrow & The Reality of Today
In our earlier issue dated 13th April, we dissected several talking points that shaped India's narrative as an alternative to China for decades to come. Read More

Despite the upward movement in stocks, risks remain elevated.

children.summary:
Last week saw some positive momentum in markets. This was because of two reasons Read More
Freight futures contango evaporates as optimism turns into caution - Not much has changed in the spot market during the past few weeks, yet Capesize futures took a dive and have now erased any premium that was present over the last few months.

children.title: Following last week's softish tone, the twentieth trading week started on the wrong foot
section_path: Following last week's softish tone, the twentieth trading week started on the wrong foot
children.section_path_text: Following last week's softish tone, the twentieth trading week started on the wrong foot
children.summary: In spite of the cheerful tone in the soybean market, the main carriers of grains in these staple seaborn runs have diverted lately from their usual flight plan, ending the week at P6_82 (ECSA RV) levels of just $12,827 daily. As a flotilla of ballasters can attest, one swallow does not a summer make neither can a single load pocket enrich the freight market overall.

children.title: Growth has in fact returned
children.summary:
As we highlighted in a recent Weekly Dry Bulk Report earlier this month, Brazil exported a total of 25.2 million tons of iron ore in April. Read More

children.title: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
section_path: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.section_path_text: Snapshot of Spot Freight Rates, Supply-Demand Trends, Port Congestions
children.summary: In the closing days of May, the freight market experienced a notable slowdown in momentum, primarily observed in the Capesize segment. Conversely, the smaller vessel size segments have maintained a relatively stable outlook.
